title: Nagano Prefecture Neo Tokyo 2
wikitext:
[]: Nagano Prefecture, Neo Tokyo 2             Quote Post by Paintballer » Sun Jul 18, 2010 6:01 am Whats with this part in EoE? What is old tokyo? Why dont they want touch for atleast 20 years after they blow up nerv? Whats up with that? ....We're not hosting a intergalactic kegger

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:17 am Old Tokyo - what we would call Tokyo today - got partly drowned by Second Impact and then apparently nuked during the subsequent chaos/war. It's apparently barren enough by 2015 that they can use it as a testing ground for thoroughly dangerous weaponry (JA) with only minor repercussions. The scene with the Prime Minister is him essentially giving the military authority to use nuclear weaponry against NERV and the Evas if they have to. There's not much of Tokyo-3 left by the time EoE takes place, however, so his line does wind up feeling a little silly. Of course if the military ever did actually request a nuclear strike they got beaten to the punch by the Mass Production Series, and it's not clear that a nuke would be any more effective against an AT field than an N2 device is.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Jul 18, 2010 5:51 pm Azathoth wrote: Of course if the military ever did actually request a nuclear strike they got beaten to the punch by the Mass Production Series, and it's not clear that a nuke would be any more effective against an AT field than an N2 device is.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sun Jul 18, 2010 7:10 pm Do you have any idea how radiation works? Off the top of my head... It kills you by screwing up the chemical interactions in the cells of your body, screwing with your DNA, and doing all manner of horrible things to your intestines, bone marrow and brain. Also consider that about 5 Grays of radiation can kill a human.... a really nasty lingering death that takes weeks. There are plenty of critters on this planet than can survive a hell of a lot more without even blinking at it. How can radiation kill an Angel, when for all intends and purposes their bodies are so radically different from our own? Have you considered the sheer size of an Angel aswell? The amount of radiation energy alonerequired to kill any Angel other than Kawaoru would be massive. And if you're close enough to an nuclear bomb to be putting that much radiation energy into it's body, it's got bigger problems.... like not just being vapourised outright. Never mind that angels operate using physics beyond human ken... they are radiation, in a way. Also, have you any idea how nuclear weapons work? Fallout from an Atomic weapon decays faster than that released from a nuclear reactor. The isotopes are different, as are the types and strength of the radiation. It might not be entirely liveable, but in a wartime situation, it'd certainly be doable to keep an irradiated Tokyo-3 operational. Now then... how effective will a nuke be against an Angel? What seems to be happening to Angels when they get N2'd? Would everyone agree with me that most of their outer surface is burned off, but otherwise the core of their bodies is unharmed. We see this with Sachiel, and with Israfeal. They're mildly scorched by about 50 kilotonnes or so. The blast effects don't seem to bother them. Blast overpressure that kills people by rupturing the air spaces in their bodies and generally turning their hollow parts to jelly isn't going to bother an Angel that is essentially solid meat and liquid. with little to no density transitions, like from muscle to kidney to stomach, It'll just pass straight through it and do it no harm. The radiation... well... it seems Angels are immune to that. So all we have is the thermal effects of the blast. How much damage can the heat given off by the explosion do? First, it'll only damage the part of the Angel facing the bomb. all the thermal radiation will strike the Angels skin, superheating it, charring it, and causing it to blast off in a cloud of soot and char that will protect the materials beneath. It ablates away, carrying the heat with it. (Like space capsule heatshields before the shuttle) Still, most of the core of the Angel will be unharmed. You'd need a massive amount of sustained heat to do more than scorch the thing. So.... use a bigger bomb. Use *more* bombs. Nuking an Angel to death is feasable. The slight problem with setting off a couple of megatonnes of nuke in Tokyo-3 is that people live and work there, and aren't going to be too please if they're homes and everything withing a 10km radius is blown to pieces every couple of weeks. Think the EVA's stomp a lot when they're out on the town, consider what a few megatonnes would do, compare.... and decide which you'd rather use. Finally, back to the original topic. ...like old Tokyo. The Prime Minister is just being sentimental. The *last* war brought by human hatred destroyed old Tokyo city, which has lain abandoned since then, because it's a flooded ruin, and it would be easier to build a new city, than rebuild the old. This next war brought about by human hatred, will do the same.

[]:             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Mon Jul 19, 2010 8:32 pm It's sure handy to have someone who knows how these things work on the board. It's a bit too bad that they are in fact just theories and can't be proved completeley. The angels might have organs, or are capable of changing their structure as well. It's logic for them to have organs since they're Adam's children. The EVA's are clones of Adam and have all organs, so assuming that the angels don't have them isn't entireley corrrect. Back to topic please

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Jul 19, 2010 10:16 pm Paintballer wrote: Whats with this part in EoE? What is old tokyo? Why dont they want touch for atleast 20 years after they blow up nerv?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jul 20, 2010 12:09 am Uh... N2 mines don't have fallout. They're NON-NUCLEAR. Here's the conversation: Prime Minister: The Instrumentality Project that NERV was secretly researching... Initiating Third Impact to annihilate mankind... The mere thought sends shivers down my spine. Secretary: Humans must be the only creatures able to despise their own kind. Prime Minister: All that's left is to take care of the NERV headquarters facilities. Secretary: Do you want Germany or China to undertake the re-development? Prime Minister: No - We won't be taken advantage of. Seal it up for 20 years, just like old Tokyo. It's just an excuse to keep anyone from being able to get in there and start planning 3I all over again. It just doesn't succeed.

[]: I think I'm confused. I mean, again.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Tue Jul 20, 2010 10:43 am Legendary wrote: Uh... N2 mines don't have fallout. They're NON-NUCLEAR.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jul 20, 2010 2:45 pm Yes. But it's N^2, meaning there's two N's. The OTHER N stands for "Non-". This is why they have no problem "nuking" that city so close to Tokyo-3 in Episode 1 (the radiation would be carried in by the wind), Israfel in Episode 9 (same), planning to "nuke" Tokyo-3 itself in Episode 16 (the fallout would have made it difficult to extricate people from shelters), and Rei using a "nuke" in Episode 19 (directly over their emergency food and water supply, no less).

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Tue Jul 20, 2010 3:06 pm I remember having a really long discussion about how N^2 bomb really work. The conclusion was following. N^2-bomb is a thermonuclear warhead. Unlike the "classic" modern thermonuclear weapons, the fusion reaction is initiated without the help of an atomic detonator. Removal of the atomic bomb from the construction of thermonuclear charge allows to avoid radioactive contamination by heavy long-lived nuclides. Actually i suspect even IRL there should be a research on creating such bomb. The new "clean" thermonuclear weapon would be incredibly tempting for the military departments of all countries.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 20, 2010 3:18 pm Old thread for N2 discussion: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=4582

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Tue Jul 20, 2010 4:00 pm Velefor wrote: I remember having a really long discussion about how N^2 bomb really work. The conclusion was following. N^2-bomb is a thermonuclear warhead. Unlike the "classic" modern thermonuclear weapons, the fusion reaction is initiated without the help of an atomic detonator. Removal of the atomic bomb from the construction of thermonuclear charge allows to avoid radioactive contamination by heavy long-lived nuclides. Actually i suspect even IRL there should be a research on creating such bomb. The new "clean" thermonuclear weapon would be incredibly tempting for the military departments of all countries.
